mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is turning down.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing periods do not deliver higher 20-day returns than non-easing periods, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised lower.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE jump episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional Rank IC is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in RSAFS and HOUST signals weakening consumer demand and housing turnover, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as revenue growth expectations fade.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is no better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to a stable or lower unemployment rate indicates stronger real output without matching labor stress, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and flat-to-lower UNRATE do not outperform baseline 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains high, real policy rates tighten and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend rises while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes fail to produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition does not outperform a model using CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count after prior hiring strength marks labor-market deterioration, which should translate into weaker S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through lower consumption and profit expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines on a 2-month moving basis
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS decline episodes do not precede negative 60-day returns more often than chance, or if the signal's out-of-sample hit rate stays below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after a prior contraction signals reopening or inventory restocking, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings revisions turn positive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive after a prior quarterly decline
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebound quarters are not followed by above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the response flips negative in more than one major rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong INDPRO combined with stable CPIAUCSL implies growth without renewed inflation pressure, supporting higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as margins and multiples both hold up.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL stays within its prior 3-month range
failure_condition: Reject if strong-output/low-inflation episodes do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if positive returns occur equally often when CPIAUCSL is accelerating.
